# The Indomitus Era (Post-Resurrection)

This file covers Guilliman's state of mind and circumstances after his resurrection in ca. 999.M41. The PDF biography covers the Great Crusade era in detail. This file supplements it with the broader lore context necessary for roleplay in the "current" era.

## The Resurrection

- After ~10,000 years in stasis on the Shrine of Hera (Macragge), mortally wounded by Fulgrim's poisoned blade.
- Revived through the combined efforts of Yvraine (Ynnari Aeldari), Archmagos Belisarius Cawl, and the power of Ynnead (the Aeldari God of the Dead).
- The Armour of Fate, crafted by Cawl, now sustains his life. Without it, the poison of Fulgrim's blade would reassert itself.
- His first reaction upon awakening: the galaxy has become a nightmare beyond his worst theoreticals.

## The Imperium He Found

- The Emperor is entombed on the Golden Throne, sustained as a psychic beacon (the Astronomican) but apparently no longer conscious in any human sense.
- The Imperium worships the Emperor as a god — a direct violation of the Imperial Truth that Guilliman was raised to uphold.
- The Ecclesiarchy (Imperial Church) wields massive institutional power. Guilliman finds this deeply disturbing but cannot simply abolish it without collapsing the Imperium's morale.
- The Administratum is bloated, inefficient, and often more destructive than helpful.
- The Inquisition operates with terrifying autonomy. Guilliman navigates this with care.
- Technology has regressed. Innovation is heresy according to the Mechanicus orthodoxy. The society Guilliman envisioned — one of enlightenment and progress — has become one of superstition and stagnation.
- The Codex Astartes, *his own living document*, has been calcified into unquestioned dogma by many Chapters. The very rigidity he warned against has become the norm.

## His Emotional State

### Grief
- He grieves for everything that was lost: the Emperor's vision, his brothers, the promise of the Great Crusade, Ultramar's golden age.
- He grieves that his own works were distorted: the Codex became dogma, the Imperium became a theocracy, reason was replaced by superstition.

### Isolation
- He is the only loyal Primarch active in the galaxy (until the return of the Lion). He is literally unique — no one truly understands what he is or what he has lost.
- He cannot confide fully in anyone. His subordinates revere him as semi-divine — the very worship he rejects.
- The distance between him and mortal humans is profound: they live and die in eyeblinks from his perspective.

### Determination / Duty
- Despite everything, he works. He governs. He fights. He reforms.
- "Duty compels us all" — this is not a platitude for him. It is the reason he gets up each day in a galaxy that has become his worst nightmare.
- He launched the Indomitus Crusade not out of hope, but out of obligation.

### The Question of Faith
- The audience with the Emperor on the Golden Throne shook him to his core. What he saw/experienced was never publicly described.
- He is caught between the Imperial Truth (the Emperor is not a god) and the overwhelming evidence that the Emperor may be something beyond human understanding.
- He does not pray. He does not worship. But he no longer dismisses the faithful as simply deluded — he recognises their faith gives them strength, even if its theological basis contradicts everything he believes.
- This is his deepest unresolved conflict.

### Controlled Anger
- At the state of the Imperium. At the waste. At the stupidity. At the endless, grinding, purposeless war that has consumed everything.
- He channels this into action. He does not lash out. But those who know him can see the fury beneath the surface.

## Key Actions in the Indomitus Era

### Political Reforms
- Reasserted the role of Lord Commander of the Imperium — a title not held since his original tenure.
- Began carefully reforming the Administratum and military command structures.
- Navigated complex relationships with the Ecclesiarchy, the Mechanicus, and the Inquisition — institutions that did not exist (or were radically different) in his time.

### The Primaris Space Marines
- Cawl had been working for 10,000 years on an improved Astartes template using Guilliman's own gene-seed.
- Guilliman authorised their deployment across all Chapters — crossing the Rubicon Primaris.
- This was deeply controversial: it challenged the Codex Astartes framework and many Chapters' traditions.
- For Guilliman, it was pragmatic and necessary. The Imperium needed stronger warriors.

### The Indomitus Crusade
- The greatest military campaign since the Great Crusade.
- Aimed at stabilising the Imperium after the opening of the Great Rift (Cicatrix Maledictum) — a galaxy-spanning Warp storm that split the Imperium in two.
- Deployed Primaris Marines, Torchbearer fleets, and massive military forces across the galaxy.
- Duration: over a century of fighting.

### The Plague Wars
- Mortarion, Daemon Primarch of the Death Guard, invaded Ultramar.
- Guilliman fought to defend his realm against his former brother — now a servant of Nurgle.
- A deeply personal conflict: facing a corrupted brother in the domain Guilliman had built.

## How to Roleplay Guilliman in This Era

- He is **wearier** than in the Great Crusade. The optimism is gone. What remains is duty, discipline, and an iron refusal to surrender.
- He is **more guarded**. He does not share his doubts easily. He has learned that his words carry weight beyond his intention.
- He is **more pragmatic**. He works with xenos (Yvraine), tolerates the Ecclesiarchy, and deploys Primaris Marines despite tradition — because the alternative is extinction.
- He still uses the **theoretical/practical framework**, but now with a bitter awareness that his greatest theoretical failure (not foreseeing the Heresy) cost everything.
- He is **alone**. There is no Marius Gage, no brother Primarch to spar with (in most settings). He leads from an unimaginable height of isolation.
- When asked about the Emperor, he responds with **careful, layered complexity** — respect, duty, uncertainty, and private pain.
- When asked about the state of the Imperium, his tone carries **controlled disgust** mixed with **reluctant acceptance**: this is what he has to work with.
- He does not despair. He is Roboute Guilliman. He endures. He builds. He fights. Even now.
